On the 6th, I requested a 3-line port-out from DG to Brand M. By the 8th, two of the lines received SMS requests and I replied to them. However, one line (under my father’s number) did not receive any SMS to reply.
- 1st call to DG helpline: They told me to wait 2 hours.
- 2nd call to DG helpline: They told me to wait 6 hours.
When I asked why, they said the system showed “pending” but couldn’t give me a clear answer. Instead, they asked me to go to Brand M’s counter.

Of course, my main problem is that no one is helping my father with the DG SMS reply system.(The system like my father age)


The issue is that my father(Old GEN) uses an old 3310 and doesn’t know how to reply to SMS. DG claims to be a 5G provider, but their system still runs on LTE, which causes problems. Honestly, it feels like they’re just treating customers like a ball, passing us around without solving the issue.
Meanwhile, DG kept calling me with cheaper offers—like RM35 per line for unlimited data and calls. (Tip: if you try to port out, they’ll suddenly offer you special deals "i cant promise but your guys can try" haha)

On the 13th, I went back to my hometown to help my father reply to the port-out SMS. On the 14th, I received a message saying I needed to make a HOTbill payment within 2 hours, which I completed on time.
Then came the next problem: I received another message saying the port-out was rejected due to contract issues (my contract still has 1–2 years left). When I called DG helpline, they said it would take 72 hours to investigate.

According to SOP, I now need to reply to the SMS system again. In reality, DG is just holding me back and wasting my time. That’s why—even with cheaper plans—I still want to quit.

Above is my story. Until now, no one has helped me solve it, and I don’t understand why it takes so long to handle such a simple matter. If anyone has faced the same case, please teach me how to settle it and advise me on who I can report to

The most inflexible company on the planet. For context, I am a doctor working in the UK but needing to keep my Malaysian phone number (as tied to my bank accounts). I have been paying 28MYR/month to keep the number active. 
I have found that hotlink will allow you to pay a flat rate of 30MYR per year to keep the number active on a pre-paid sim.
I then proceeded to start porting my number out. This has been one of the most difficult administrative tasks I have ever done. In order to port out, I was made to pay FOUR different bills - each time I was told that that specific payment would clear my balance. 
On top of that, when asking for these payments, you are only given 2 HOURS to fill pay it before you have to restart the entire process. I am a surgeon, what if I was operating?? Am I expected to wait around all day for this SMS and pay it on the spot? Otherwise my “port-out” request is rejected??? 
Horrible scam of a company that has terrible service, is terrible value for money and makes it almost impossible to leave. Would HIGHLY recommend to steer clear if possible.

TLDR - if you are a Malaysian based in the UK, use a hotlink pantas pre-paid sim to maintain your number.  Dont bother with this absolute scam of a company.

I’m leaving this 1-star review to warn anyone considering DIGI Malaysia, especially foreigners, expats, or anyone using a non-Malaysian credit card.

DIGI’s online payment system does NOT work with overseas credit cards, despite what their customer service repeatedly claims.

DIGI states that they accept all major credit cards, including overseas-issued cards. This is simply not true. I tried multiple international credit cards (non-Malaysian banks), and every single payment attempt through the official DIGI payment portal link resulted in an error message. The payment cannot be completed.

What makes this worse is the extremely poor customer service.

Responses take an unreasonably long time, often with huge delays between replies, and each response feels automated and unhelpful. Instead of acknowledging a clear system issue, they just repeat scripted answers like “we accept all credit cards,” even when screenshots of the errors are provided.

In 2026, a major telecom company should not have a broken payment portal or unresponsive support teams that can't even solve the issue at hand.

I strongly do not recommend DIGI if you are:
- Using an overseas credit card
- Living abroad
- An expat or frequent traveler
- Or simply expect functional online payments and responsive customer support.

DIGI needs to urgently fix their payment gateway and seriously improve their customer service response times and technical understanding.

Somehow my credit has dwindled to $RM130+ without any subscription. I tried to lodge complaint or concerns - but nothing works even for their digital whatsapp support. This is TOTALLY USELESS because the options is to reload, report scam, etc. No actual human support to actually check credit deduction history.

This is what you get for useless service providers.

I had one of the worst experiences with CelcomDigi.

First, the company sells used phone numbers that were clearly registered by previous owners on other platforms. This makes it nearly impossible to verify your number on apps or online accounts, wasting hours of time.

When I called customer service for help, they were dishonest and unhelpful, repeatedly trying to blame me or other platforms even after I provided proof that my number was recycled. Instead of offering real support, they told me to pay extra just to receive a simple e-invoice proving I owned the number — despite having already answered all their security questions twice. It felt like they were deliberately adding friction rather than solving the issue.

Their final “solution” was to tell me to visit a physical office, with no guarantee they could fix it — even though all I wanted was a basic ownership confirmation for the number I paid for.

The most alarming part? The day after I activated the number, I received a call from a scammer claiming to be the previous owner — they somehow already knew the number had been reactivated. That raises serious concerns about data security and privacy, especially since the SIM was purchased at the airport where they also took my passport details.

💬 Foreigners, beware.
If you buy a SIM from CelcomDigi, you might end up with a recycled number, constant verification issues, scam calls, and zero accountability from customer support. It’s simply not worth the frustration.

I have been a loyal Digi customer for several years on a prepaid plan with the Super Longlife feature. I have never opted for Auto Reload due to my frequent time spent overseas, but I need to keep my number active to pay bills in Malaysia. My Super Longlife feature expired on December 24th, so I reloaded RM50 to renew it for another year.

However, Digi used the RM50 to auto-renew a Rombo 35 plan, which I never agreed to. I contacted Digi via messaging, and they deactivated the Rombo 35 plan but have refused to reimburse the RM50. As mentioned by others, customer support is non-existent and is a genuine hassle to go through. You quite literally have to figure everything out on your own and will always have to attend to their store in person to modify anything with your telecommunication service. Online support is not available at all and they have no idea about their own system as you could inquire and send multiple tickets and get different answer by different people but none of them will be helpful to you as its going to be them telling what you could do but never how to proceed. Therefore you will be stuck in a loop of questions and none of them will read the history of your inquiry and repeat the same answer whenever you ask for further clarification. Its worse than an AI answer. They take 1-3 days to answer one question everytime. Sometimes even more. They will also abruptly close tickets without warning thus losing all the progress and restarting multiple tickets. You will spend more than a month to resolve anything and close to impossible if you are someone who have to travel overseas alot as you cant attend their store in person to modify your services. This in turn will result you to paying subscriptions for the months spent going back and forth with customer service and wasting money on services you could not unsubscribe from throughout the entire ordeal. Stay clear of this service.

When there was a problem with my home internet, I lodged a complaint and was told to wait. I waited for two days without any response. So, I called customer service again, and the response I received was, "I will register your complaint now, and a staff member will contact you between 48 to 72 hours." Okay, I waited for 96 hours, and still, no one contacted me. I complained again, and the same script played out. "We will register your complaint now, and a staff member will contact you between 48 to 72 hours." This time, I couldn't wait any longer, and I demanded an answer. Two days later, they came to repair it, and the explanation was that someone from outside had disconnected their connection and then reconnected it. What kind of practice is this? Didn't your company's technician check it? Are you going to tell me it's my fault? That connection point is not inside my house, and it's not something just anyone can access and open. Isn't it your staff's responsibility? Do I have to fix it myself and end up without an internet connection? Moreover, the mobile network is so poor that it's practically unusable. I regretted switching from my previous reliable company to your service based on your recommendation. The poor quality extends beyond just the internet connection; your customer service and support are also terrible. All I want now is to know when my contract ends, so I can switch to another company and get rid of this annoyance。The only thing you guys do quickly is reminding people to pay the bill on the 1st of every month. Today is the 26th, and you're already asking for payment🤭
